content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be restructured around a recursive self-modeling principle where the agent itself becomes the core organizing structure for its own active learning memory, rather than treating memory as a separate storage module attached to the agent. This recursive framework lets the agent actively prioritize and curate memory updates by aligning them to its evolving curiosity-driven goals, with the agent’s self-representation acting as the query engine for active learning sampling.
